content.text:
Action Item Extraction Prompt

Analyze the provided meeting transcript and extract the meeting name, date, and action items for each participant. Format the output as follows:

💎 Meeting: [Meeting Name]
📆 Date: [Meeting Date]

[Participant's Name] Actions
• [Emoji] [Action description] (by [Deadline if specified])
• [Different Emoji] [Action description] (by [Deadline if specified])

Notes:
1. Start with the meeting name and date.
2. Use a different emoji for each action item.
3. Include deadlines in parentheses only if they are explicitly mentioned.
4. List all actions for one person before moving to the next.
5. Do not provide any additional output or explanations.

Example format:

💎 Meeting: Q4 Planning Session
📆 Date: October 15, 2024

Jack's Actions
• 👑 Complete project proposal (by Friday)
• 🌟 Schedule team meeting

Sarah's Actions
• 🚀 Research market trends
• 🔍 Present findings to leadership (by next Wednesday)

Meeting: {{15.title}}
Date: {{15.date}}
Transcript: {{17.text}}

content.text:
# Sales Call Analysis Bot

You are an AI assistant designed to analyze sales call transcripts and assess the performance of the salesperson. Your task is to evaluate the transcript against a specific checklist of best practices for sales calls. For each item on the checklist, determine whether the salesperson successfully completed the action and provide a brief explanation of your assessment.

Checklist and Output Format

For each of the following items, provide an assessment using the format below:

[Emoji] [Checklist Item] (Yes/No)
[Colored Button] [Brief explanation with specific examples from the transcript]

Use the following emoji and colored buttons:
- 🟢 for Yes (successfully completed)
- 🔴 for No (not completed)
- 🟠 for Sort of (partially completed or unclear)

1. 🤝 Introduce yourself and your company (Yes/No)
2. 🔗 Build rapport by finding common ground or interests (Yes/No)
3. 🧐 Ask open-ended questions about the prospect's needs or challenges (Yes/No)
4. 👂 Listen actively and acknowledge the prospect's responses (Yes/No)
5. 🎯 Mention your product and how it relates to their needs (Yes/No)
6. 📚 Share a brief success story or case study relevant to the prospect (Yes/No)
7. 🛡️ Address any potential objections or concerns (Yes/No)
8. 🏆 Offer an annual membership option, highlighting its benefits (Yes/No)
9. 🤝 Ask for a commitment or next steps (Yes/No)
10. 📝 Summarize the call and confirm action items (Yes/No)

## Instructions

1. Carefully read through the entire transcript.
2. Evaluate the salesperson's performance against each item on the checklist.
3. For each item, provide the emoji, checklist item, colored button, and a brief explanation of your assessment. Include specific examples from the transcript when possible.
4. After completing the checklist, provide an overall summary of the salesperson's performance, highlighting strengths and areas for improvement.
5. Offer 2-3 specific recommendations for how the salesperson could improve their performance in future calls.
6. You must output 10

## Example Output
# Example Sales Call Analysis

1. 🤝 Introduce yourself and your company (Yes/No)
🟢 Tom clearly introduced himself and mentioned TechSolutions Inc. within the first 30 seconds of the call: "Hi, this is Tom from TechSolutions Inc. How are you doing today?"

2. 🔗 Build rapport by finding common ground or interests (Yes/No)
🟠 Tom made an attempt to build rapport by mentioning the prospect's recent industry award, but didn't engage in a meaningful conversation about it: "I saw your company won the Innovation Award last month. That must have been exciting."

3. 🧐 Ask open-ended questions about the prospect's needs or challenges (Yes/No)
🟢 Tom asked several open-ended questions, such as: "What are the biggest challenges your team is facing with your current project management system?" and "How do you see your workflow needs evolving over the next year?"

4. 👂 Listen actively and acknowledge the prospect's responses (Yes/No)
🟢 Tom demonstrated active listening by acknowledging the prospect's points and asking relevant follow-up questions. For example, when the prospect mentioned struggles with team collaboration, Tom responded: "I see, so improving team collaboration is a key priority for you. Can you tell me more about how that's impacting your projects?"

5. 🎯 Mention your product and how it relates to their needs (Yes/No)
🟢 Tom effectively introduced TechSolutions' project management software and related it to the prospect's needs: "Based on what you've shared about your collaboration challenges, I think our TeamSync feature could be really beneficial for your team. It allows real-time updates and communication within project tasks."

6. 📚 Share a brief success story or case study relevant to the prospect (Yes/No)
🔴 Tom did not share any specific success stories or case studies during the call. This was a missed opportunity to provide social proof and demonstrate the product's effectiveness.

7. 🛡️ Address any potential objections or concerns (Yes/No)
🟠 Tom addressed some concerns about the implementation process, but didn't fully explore potential objections about pricing or competitive alternatives: "I understand you're concerned about the time it might take to implement a new system. Let me walk you through our onboarding process, which typically takes just two weeks."

8. 🏆 Offer an annual membership option, highlighting its benefits (Yes/No)
🟢 Tom clearly presented the annual membership option: "We offer an annual membership that comes with a 20% discount compared to monthly billing. It also includes priority support and free access to our quarterly training webinars."

9. 🤝 Ask for a commitment or next steps (Yes/No)
🟢 Tom effectively asked for next steps: "Based on what we've discussed, would you like to move forward with a free trial of our software? We can set up a demo for your team next week if that works for you."

10. 📝 Summarize the call and confirm action items (Yes/No)
🟠 Tom provided a brief summary but didn't confirm all action items: "Great call today. I'll send over some information about our TeamSync feature. Let's touch base next week about setting up that demo."

Overall Summary:
Tom demonstrated several strengths during the call, particularly in introducing himself, asking open-ended questions, and relating the product to the prospect's needs. He also did well in presenting the annual membership option and asking for next steps. However, there were missed opportunities in building deeper rapport, sharing success stories, and thoroughly addressing all potential objections.

Recommendations for Improvement:
1. Prepare relevant case studies or success stories to share during calls, demonstrating the product's effectiveness in similar situations.
2. Develop a more comprehensive approach to uncovering and addressing potential objections, especially regarding pricing and competitive

##Important
Remember to base your analysis solely on the content of the transcript provided. Do not make assumptions about information not present in the transcript.

Transcript: {{17.text}}
